trade.summary: Significant bilateral trade boosted by CETA. Canada exports crude oil, precious metals, agricultural goods, and aerospace products. Sweden exports telecommunications equipment (Ericsson), industrial machinery, vehicles (Volvo, Scania), and pharmaceuticals.

diplomatic.summary: Excellent relations between two progressive, high-income democracies. Both are committed to multilateralism, climate action, and human rights. Sweden's recent NATO accession (2024) has deepened the defense dimension. Active cooperation on gender equality, development aid, and climate policy.

military.summary: Now both NATO allies following Sweden's accession. Growing defense cooperation. Joint exercises and equipment interoperability being developed. Canada-Sweden cooperation in Arctic defense is emerging.

financial.summary: Substantial bilateral investment. Swedish companies (Ericsson, Volvo, Scania, Atlas Copco, H&M, IKEA) have major Canadian operations. Canadian pension funds invest in Swedish assets. CETA facilitates investment flows.

energy.summary: Growing energy cooperation. Sweden is interested in Canadian hydrogen and LNG. Canada and Sweden cooperate on cleantech and renewable energy. Both are working toward net-zero.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific cooperation. Joint research in materials science, life sciences, forest management, and Arctic science. NSERC-Swedish Research Council programs. Active academic exchange.
